botResponse: Hey there! It's completely normal to
It's totally understandable to feel that way sometimes! Everyone struggles with focus, and

Hey there! It's completely understandable to feel frustrated when focus is hard to find. Many students go through this, and it doesn't mean you're not capable.

Here are a couple of quotes that might offer a little lift when you're feeling stuck:

"The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step." - Lao Tzu.
This reminds us that even just opening your book or reading one sentence is a start! Don't feel you need to achieve everything at once.

"You don't have to be great to start, but you have to start to be great." - Zig Ziglar.
It’s about taking that first small action, no matter how small it feels. Try setting a timer for just 15 minutes and focus on one tiny task. You've got this!

userMessage: i am 3rd year student and i am worrying about my end sem please guide me
botResponse:
Hey there! It's completely normal to feel worried about end-semester exams, especially as a 3rd-year student. Many feel the same way!

Remember, "You are capable of more than you know." Try breaking down your study material into smaller, manageable chunks. This makes it less overwhelming. Also, schedule short, regular breaks – a fresh mind learns better!

As Eleanor Roosevelt said, "The future belongs to those who believe in the beauty of their dreams." Trust in your hard work so far. Focus on what you can control, like your study schedule and taking care of yourself. You've got this!
